dependencies.description:
XLA library for Jax. jaxlib is the support library for JAX. While JAX
itself is a pure Python package, jaxlib contains the binary (C/C++)
parts of the library, including Python bindings, the XLA compiler, the
PJRT runtime, and a handful of handwritten kernels.
dependencies.name: py-jaxlib

dependencies.description:
A stand-alone implementation of several NumPy dtype extensions used in
machine learning libraries.
dependencies.name: py-ml-dtypes

dependencies.description: Fundamental package for array computing in Python.
dependencies.name: py-numpy

dependencies.description: Optimized Einsum: A tensor contraction order optimizer.
dependencies.name: py-opt-einsum

dependencies.description: The PyPA recommended tool for installing Python packages.
dependencies.name: py-pip

dependencies.description: Fundamental algorithms for scientific computing in Python.
dependencies.name: py-scipy

dependencies.description:
A Python utility that aids in the process of downloading, building,
upgrading, installing, and uninstalling Python packages.
dependencies.name: py-setuptools

dependencies.description: A Spack managed Python virtual environment
dependencies.name: python-venv

dependent_to.description:
AlphaFold is an AI system developed by DeepMind that predicts a
protein's 3D structure from its amino acid sequence. It regularly
achieves accuracy competitive with experiment.
dependent_to.name: py-alphafold

dependent_to.description:
APEBench is a JAX-based tool to evaluate autoregressive neural emulators
for PDEs on periodic domains in 1d, 2d, and 3d. It comes with an
efficient reference simulator based on spectral methods that is used for
procedural data generation (no need to download large datasets with
APEBench). Since this simulator can also be embedded into emulator
training (e.g., for a "solver-in-the-loop" correction setting), this is
the first benchmark suite to support differentiable physics.
dependent_to.name: py-apebench

dependent_to.description: Chex is a library of utilities for helping to write reliable JAX code.
dependent_to.name: py-chex

dependent_to.description: Dinosaur: differentiable dynamics for global atmospheric modeling.
dependent_to.name: py-dinosaur

dependent_to.description: JAX-based neural network library
dependent_to.name: py-dm-haiku

dependent_to.description:
Earth-2 Model Intercomparison Project (MIP). A python framework that
enables climate researchers and scientists to explore and experiment
with AI models for weather and climate.
dependent_to.name: py-earth2mip

dependent_to.description:
Equinox is your one-stop [JAX](https://github.com/google/jax) library,
for everything you need that isn't already in core JAX: - neural
networks (or more generally any model), with easy-to-use PyTorch-like
syntax; - filtered APIs for transformations; - useful PyTree
manipulation routines; - advanced features like runtime errors; and best
of all, Equinox isn't a framework: everything you write in Equinox is
compatible with anything else in JAX or the ecosystem.
dependent_to.name: py-equinox

dependent_to.description: Efficient Differentiable n-d PDE solvers built on top of JAX & Equinox.
dependent_to.name: py-exponax

dependent_to.description: Flax: A neural network library for JAX designed for flexibility.
dependent_to.name: py-flax

dependent_to.description: GraphCast: Learning skillful medium-range global weather forecasting.
dependent_to.name: py-graphcast

dependent_to.description: JMP is a Mixed Precision library for JAX.
dependent_to.name: py-jmp

dependent_to.description: Jraph: A library for Graph Neural Networks in Jax.
dependent_to.name: py-jraph

dependent_to.description:
Multi-backend Keras. Keras 3 is a multi-backend deep learning framework,
with support for JAX, TensorFlow, PyTorch, and OpenVINO (for inference-
only).
dependent_to.name: py-keras

dependent_to.description:
Zero-copy MPI communication of JAX arrays, for turbo-charged HPC
applications in Python.
dependent_to.name: py-mpi4jax

dependent_to.description: NeuralGCM: Hybrid ML + Physics model of Earth's atmosphere.
dependent_to.name: py-neuralgcm

dependent_to.description: Use numba-compiled kernels from within Jax
dependent_to.name: py-numba4jax

dependent_to.description: A gradient processing and optimisation library in JAX.
dependent_to.name: py-optax

dependent_to.description:
Orbax includes a checkpointing library oriented towards JAX users,
supporting a variety of different features required by different
frameworks, including asynchronous checkpointing various types, and
various storage formats. We aim to provide a highly customizable and
composable API which maximizes flexibility for diverse use cases.
dependent_to.name: py-orbax-checkpoint

dependent_to.description:
A collection of neural architectures for emulating Partial Differential
Equations (PDEs) in JAX agnostic to the spatial dimension (1D, 2D, 3D)
and boundary conditions (Dirichlet, Neumann, Periodic). This package is
built on top of Equinox.
dependent_to.name: py-pdequinox

dependent_to.description: TensorFlow is an open source machine learning framework for everyone.
dependent_to.name: py-tensorflow

dependent_to.description:
TensorFlow Probability (TFP) is a Python library built on TensorFlow
that makes it easy to combine probabilistic models and deep learning on
modern hardware (TPU, GPU). It's for data scientists, statisticians, ML
researchers, and practitioners who want to encode domain knowledge to
understand data and make predictions.
dependent_to.name: py-tensorflow-probability

dependent_to.description:
Convenience abstractions using optax to train neural networks to
autoregressively emulate time-dependent problems taking care of
trajectory subsampling and offering a wide range of training
methodologies (regarding unrolling length and including differentiable
physics).
dependent_to.name: py-trainax

dependent_to.description: Mathematical operations for JAX pytrees.
dependent_to.name: py-tree-math

description:
Differentiate, compile, and transform Numpy code. JAX is a Python
library for accelerator-oriented array computation and program
transformation, designed for high-performance numerical computing and
large-scale machine learning.
name: py-jax

variants.description: Build systems supported by the package
variants.name: build_system
